pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the SAE_Maintenance arcade cabinet project, summarizing its architecture, technology stack, and how the major subsystems relate to each other.

pages.title: Setup and Boot
pages.purpose: Describe how the arcade cabinet is provisioned and started, covering both the dependency installation script and the runtime boot orchestration script.

pages.title: Environment Provisioning
pages.purpose: Document setup-raspberry.sh in detail: what it installs (Java 17, Python 3, SDL2, pygame, LOVE 2D, xdotool, librosa, numpy, scipy), how it compiles each game project, and how it sets executable permissions.

pages.title: Boot Sequence
pages.purpose: Document lancerBorne.sh step by step: keyboard layout setup, pre-launch cleanup, compilation, Java menu launch, post-exit cleanup, re-provisioning, and the 30-second countdown before sudo halt.

pages.title: Main Menu Application
pages.purpose: Describe the Java menu application that runs on boot, combining graphical rendering, game discovery, music playback, user navigation, and game launching into a single kiosk frontend.

pages.title: Graphical Interface
pages.purpose: Document Graphique and its supporting components: full-screen window initialization via FenetrePleinEcran, dynamic game discovery from projet/ subdirectories, background music management, the BoiteImage preview, BoiteDescription, BoiteSelection navigation, and the Bouton game entry type.

pages.title: Game Selection and Launch
pages.purpose: Document the Pointeur class: how it tracks the selected game index, renders selection indicators, and implements lancerJeu to parse and execute game shell scripts line by line, including the cd/touch/xdotool/executeCommand dispatch logic and its known limitations.

pages.title: Input Handling
pages.purpose: Document the ClavierBorneArcade class that appears across multiple game directories: how it implements KeyListener, tracks pressed (Enfoncee) and tapped (Tape) states for joystick directions and A/B/C/X/Y/Z buttons for both players, and provides reinitialisation. Also covers the Minesweeper-specific KeyboardArcade variant and the shared TestClavierBorneArcade test class.

pages.title: MG2D Graphics Library
pages.purpose: Document the MG2D library bundled with the project: its key classes (FenetrePleinEcran, Texture, Point, Couleur, Bruitage), how it is used by the menu and the Java games, and the included javazoom MP3 decoder that powers audio playback.

pages.title: Games
pages.purpose: Introduce the full collection of games available on the arcade cabinet, describe the common launcher script pattern, and explain the projet/ directory structure and high score file convention.

pages.title: Java Games
pages.purpose: Cover all Java-based games collectively, explaining the shared launcher pattern (xdotool, cd, touch highscore, javac, java) and listing each game with its directory and entry class.

pages.title: Columns
pages.purpose: Document the Columns game: its launcher script, the two-player game loop using FenetrePleinEcran and Controles, Menu and Partie state management, and the pasTemps time step.

pages.title: DinoRail
pages.purpose: Document the DinoRail game: its launcher script, the DinoRail class game loop, Obstacle entities (cactus and bird assets), collision detection via intersectionRapide, off-screen management, score display, and game-over condition.

pages.title: Minesweeper
pages.purpose: Document the Minesweeper game: its launcher, Board class (grid, bomb placement, neighbour counting, win/lose conditions), Tile, Button, Cursor, Level, the graphical entry point via MainGraphic, MinesweeperView, and the Menu component.

pages.title: Pong
pages.purpose: Document the Pong game: its launcher script, the Pong class game logic, Main entry point, and two-player input via ClavierBorneArcade.

pages.title: Other Java Games
pages.purpose: Document the remaining Java games: JavaSpace (Jeu lifecycle with majMenu/avancerUnPasDeTemps), InitialDrift, Puissance_X (with Java2D rendering notes), Snake_Eater (Pomme and Serpent components), and Kowasu_Renga.

pages.title: Python Games
pages.purpose: Document all Python-based games: OsuTile (config, game, menu, tile modules and map system), PianoTile (core player/pageState, UI interface, layout views, window manager), TronGame, and ball-blast (ball, bullet, player, game, menu modules).

pages.title: Testing and Utilities
pages.purpose: Document the test classes bundled in the root of borne_arcade: TestClavierBorneArcade for verifying arcade input mapping and TestHighScore for verifying high score read/write logic.
